prose:
**Formaldehyde frontier orbitals** — Computed with the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` task using DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) in PySCF, **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz` (4 atoms); this was a frontier-orbital single-point calculation at that geometry. SCF converged: true, in 8 cycles. No warnings were reported by the tool result. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true.

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV**.

Additional orbital results from the same calculation: HOMO = -7.6523067 eV; LUMO = -1.7826355 eV. Total electronic energy = -114.55325 Hartree = -3117.1528 eV. The HOMO–LUMO gap is a Koopmans-style orbital energy gap, not an excitation energy.

Exact chemkit command returned by the engine: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmprg1wgfae/live.json`.

The tool result did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot report one without fabricating it.
